channel.title: Context Engineering Daily
channel.description: Daily news and research updates in AI Context Engineering, Prompt Engineering, RAG, and LLM development

item.title: How Personas Can Influence Agents to Play Split or Steal
item.description: arXiv:2607.05398v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Personas are often employed to guide large language model agents, yet their effectiveness in shaping strategic behavior in social dilemma settings remains uncertain. To address this, we examined the impact of persona prompts in an iterated Split or St...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> GPT, API, large language model, model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Benchmarking KV-Cache Optimizations across Task Quality and System Performance for Long-Context Serving
item.description: arXiv:2607.05399v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model serving is increasingly limited by KV-cache growth under long-context workloads, yet existing KV-cache compression techniques are difficult to compare because they were evaluated on different models, tasks, budgets, and serving st...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, few-shot, few-shot learning, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The yes-no bias of large language models reflects answer order and wording, not shifts in moral judgment
item.description: arXiv:2607.05552v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly issue judgments read as binary verdicts, and a growing literature reports such judgments shifting under logically irrelevant changes of wording - among them an amplified yes-no bias on moral dilemmas, absent i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, LLM, GPT, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Prompt Robustness Is Task-Dependent: Comparing Objective and Belief-Style Questions in LLM Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2607.05554v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Survey-style evaluations of large language models often treat a prompted response as a measure of a model's values or beliefs. This assumption is particularly fragile when responses are read as evidence of political values, social attitudes, or belief...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, LLM, large language model, model, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ResonatorLM: Causal Resonant Field Mixing for Efficient Long-Context Language Modelin
item.description: arXiv:2607.05583v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Contemporary language models are dominated by the transformer architecture, which leverages self-attention mechanisms to enable more efficient, parallelized training across a wide set of documents and corpora. This has allowed transformers to effectiv...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, attention, product, transformer, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Revisiting the Relation Between Language Model Perplexity and ASR Word Error Rate for Modern End-to-End Speech Recognition
item.description: arXiv:2607.05612v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language model (LM) perplexity (PPL) has historically been used as a proxy for automatic speech recognition (ASR) word error rate (WER), with prior work reporting an approximately linear relation in log-log space. Modern end-to-end ASR systems challen...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, attention, LLM, large language model, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BaFCo: A Document Understanding Benchmark for Complex Bangla Form Comprehension
item.description: arXiv:2607.05614v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Document comprehension is a challenging yet impactful task for Multimodal Large Language Models, especially as these systems see growing adoption in real-world, human-centric applications. However, this adoption is limited for low-resource languages s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, zero-shot, GPT, multimodal, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: NAVER LABS System Re-implementation for the IWSLT 2026 Instruction-Following Task
item.description: arXiv:2607.05623v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We re-implement the NAVER LABS IWSLT 2025 instruction-following pipeline for the IWSLT 2026 Shared Task (constrained condition, short audio track), adapting it to the mandated components: SeamlessM4T-v2-large as the speech encoder and Qwen3-4B-Instruc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> fine-tuning, LLM, multimodal, model, instruction | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Prompt-to-Paper: Agentic AI System for Bioinformatics
item.description: arXiv:2607.05456v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While recent advances in large language models have enabled end-to-end automated manuscript generation, existing systems suffer from three critical deficiencies: (i) generated claims are not deterministically grounded in verifiable literature, (ii) ex...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, RAG, large language model, framework, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: CSTutorBench: Benchmarking Small Language Models as Tutors for Block-Based Programming
item.description: arXiv:2607.05571v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly explored as AI tutors, yet deploying them in K-12 settings raises concerns around privacy, cost, and reliance on proprietary models. Small language models (SLMs) offer a promising alternative, but selecting the r...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, vision, large language model, model, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Foundation Models for Automatic CAD Generation
item.description: arXiv:2607.05573v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advances in Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision-Language Models (VLMs) enable the automatic generation of parametric 3D designs from natural-language specifications. This chapter presents an empirical study of foundation models for automati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, vision, arxiv, large language model, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Narrative World Model: Narratology-Grounded Writer Memory for Long-Form Fiction
item.description: arXiv:2607.05577v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-form fiction writers need memory that answers multi-hop questions about evolving story state: who knows a secret and when they learned it, whether an event preceded the narration that revealed it, whether a setup paid off, and how a relationship ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, memory, framework, model, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: FirstResearch: Auditable Question Formation for LLM Scientific Discovery Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.05682v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM systems for scientific discovery increasingly assist with ideation, literature synthesis, experiment planning, and report generation, but the first research question they propose can remain difficult to audit: it may sound plausible without exposi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, LLM, product, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Memory in the Loop: In-Process Retrieval as ExtendedWorking Memory for Language Agents
item.description: arXiv:2607.05690v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language agents run a loop - observe, reason, act - but the memory they reason over sits outside it: a store queried at most once per turn. We study the regime where memory moves inside the loop, read and written on every step. The obstacle has always...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, memory, GPT, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Akashic: A Low-Overhead LLM Inference Service with MemAttention
item.description: arXiv:2607.05708v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent LLM-based agent systems continuously accumulate context across multi-turn interactions, tool invocations, and cross-session workflows. Replaying the full history for every request quickly becomes impractical: long contexts increase prefill cost...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, attention, memory, tool, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Synthetic Consumer Insight Generation with Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2607.05761v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern data-driven marketing relies on large amounts of consumer data, yet collecting such data can be costly, time-consuming, and difficult to scale. This research examines whether large language models (LLMs) can be used to generate synthetic consum...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, model, prompting, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Design-CP: Context Parallelism for Design of Protein Nanoparticles
item.description: arXiv:2607.05439v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many all-atom generative protein models can in principle design large multimeric complexes by jointly modelling all chains, but their quadratic token- and atom-pair representations quickly exceed single-GPU memory as the number of chains and residues ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, memory, ICL, model, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Exogenous Dropout: A Simple, Strong Baseline for Corruption-Robust Time Series Forecasting with Covariates
item.description: arXiv:2607.05452v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time series forecasters that use exogenous covariates are fragile in deployment: when those covariates are noised, temporally misaligned, or missing, strong exogenous-fusion and exogenous-adapted models can degrade far above the endogenous-only floor....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> release, RAG, model, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning to Control LLM Agent Harnesses with Offline Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2607.05458v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are usually improved by changing prompts, models, or hand-written workflows, while the execution harness around the model is treated as fixed infrastructure. We argue that this harness is itself a learnable control la...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, model, arxiv, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Breaking Structural Isolation: Scalable Graph Clustering via Community-Aware Sampling and Structural Entropy
item.description: arXiv:2607.05469v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Unsupervised graph clustering is a fundamental technique for uncovering underlying semantic patterns in large-scale networks. Although Graph Contrastive Learning has demonstrated promising performance, existing methods often suffer from the "structura...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, framework, context, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Native-speed vLLM transformers modeling backend

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (Hugging Face Blog)

item.title: Context-Engineering - "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.
item.description: "Context engineering is the delicate art and science of filling the context window with just the right information for the next step." — Andrej Karpathy. A frontier, first-principles handbook inspired by Karpathy and 3Blue1Brown for moving beyond prompt engineering to the wider discipline of context design, orchestration, and optimization.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, context window, prompt, prompt engineering | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: awesome-prompt-engineering - A curated list of resources, tools, papers, and platforms for prompt engineering in large language models (LLMs) and generative AI.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: ThinkSound - [NeurIPS 2025] PyTorch implementation of [ThinkSound], a unified framework for generating audio from any modality, guided by Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning.

item.title: LightRAG - [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"
item.description: [EMNLP2025] "LightRAG: Simple and Fast Retrieval-Augmented Generation"<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> retrieval, augmented, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: KAG - KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.
item.description: KAG is a logical form-guided reasoning and retrieval framework based on OpenSPG engine and LLMs. It is used to build logical reasoning and factual Q&A solutions for professional domain knowledge bases. It can effectively overcome the shortcomings of the traditional RAG vector similarity calculation model.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, LLM, knowledge base, framework, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Kiln - Build, Evaluate, and Optimize AI Systems. Includes evals, RAG, agents, fine-tuning, synthetic data generation, dataset management, MCP, and more.

item.title: R2R - SoTA production-ready AI retrieval system. Agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) with a RESTful API.

item.title: Text Distance from Nested and Hierarchical Repetitions: A Compression-Based Perspective
item.description: arXiv:2607.05416v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present a new method for structural sequence analysis grounded in Algorithmic Information Theory (AIT). At its core is the Ladderpath approach, which extracts nested and hierarchical relationships among repeated substructures in linguistic sequence...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> few-shot, model, compression, analysis, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Statistically Meaningful Geometry and Gauge Symmetry Breaking: A Geometric Foundation for Scientific Discovery and Intelligence Emergence
item.description: arXiv:2607.05436v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rapid scaling of over-parameterized machine learning architectures, particularly LLMs, raises a profound crisis: do these systems exhibit genuine intelligence, or are they merely sophisticated statistical pattern matchers? Classical flat Euclidean...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, API, framework, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learnable Weighting of Intra-Attribute Distances for Categorical Data Clustering with Nominal and Ordinal Attributes
item.description: arXiv:2607.05464v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The success of categorical data clustering generally much relies on the distance metric that measures the dissimilarity degree between two objects. However, most of the existing clustering methods treat the two categorical subtypes, i.e. nominal and o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, experiment, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: cosmos-reason1 - Cosmos-Reason1 models understand the physical common sense and generate appropriate embodied decisions in natural language through long chain-of-thought reasoning processes.

item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)

item.title: Most LLM Conformity Needs No Speaker: Measuring the Speaker-Free Floor in Peer-Pressure Benchmarks
item.description: arXiv:2607.05545v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM conformity is often used to describe cases where a model changes a correct answer toward a peer or group response. We show that most of this apparent conformity survives even after the peer is removed. The reason is a confound: standard conformity...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, vision, model, reasoning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Empirical Minimal-Realisation Compression of Deep Neural Networks via Controllability-Observability Tests
item.description: arXiv:2607.05457v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep neural networks often contain substantial hidden-state redundancy, but most compression methods operate directly on weights, neurons, or quantised representations without explicitly characterising the dynamical role of internal states. This paper...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, framework, model, compression, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: code-review-graph - Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.
item.description: Local-first code intelligence graph for MCP and CLI. Builds a persistent map of your codebase so AI coding tools read only what matters, with benchmarked context reductions on reviews and large-repo workflows.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>

item.title: Claude-Code-Everything-You-Need-to-Know - A practical Claude Code guide with clear mental models and copy-paste examples — setup, prompt engineering, slash commands, skills, hooks, subagents, agent teams, and MCP servers. Beginner path to power-user depth. Featured in Awesome Claude Code.
item.description: A practical Claude Code guide with clear mental models and copy-paste examples — setup, prompt engineering, slash commands, skills, hooks, subagents, agent teams, and MCP servers. Unlike traditional explainability met...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, framework, model, reasoning, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>

item.title: ArtisanCAD: An Industrial-Level CAD Agent with Expert-Grounded Knowledge Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2607.05750v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Computer-aided design (CAD) for industrial components requires long-horizon procedural modeling, robust feature dependencies, editable parametric geometry, and production-grade B-Rep execution. Existing text-to-CAD methods have made promising progress...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, product, model, arxiv, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Granularity Paradox: How Temporal Disaggregation Inflates In-Sample Fit and Compounds Out-of-Sample Error
item.description: arXiv:2607.05450v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper explores the "Granularity Paradox" in time-series forecasting, wherein finer temporal disaggregation (e.g., Monthly to Weekly/Daily) improves in-sample diagnostics and dataset size (N), but degrades out-of-sample accuracy due to recursive e...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AdaStop: Cost-Aware Early Stopping for DNN Test Selection
item.description: arXiv:2607.05461v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing methods for testing deep neural networks (DNNs) primarily prioritize test inputs likely to reveal model faults under a fixed labeling budget. In practice, choosing that budget is difficult: too little testing misses failures, while too much i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, experiment, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: WrenAI - GenBI (Generative BI) for AI agents, an open-source, governed text-to-SQL through an open context layer that turns natural-language questions into trusted dashboards, charts, and SQL across 20+ data sources, such as BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Amazon Redshift, Databricks and more.
item.description: GenBI (Generative BI) for AI agents, an open-source, governed text-to-SQL through an open context layer that turns natural-language questions into trusted dashboards, charts, and SQL across 20+ data sources, such as BigQuery, Snowflake, PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, Amazon Redshift, Databricks and more.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
